title: How The Enhabit (EHAB) Story Is Shifting After The Kinderhook US$13.80 Cash Offer
content:
Make better investment decisions with Simply Wall St's easy, visual tools that give you a competitive edge.

Enhabit’s fair value price target is holding at $13.80 per share, directly matching the agreed all cash acquisition price and keeping the deal front and center for investors. That alignment reflects how bullish and bearish analyst views now cluster around the offer level, with prior optimism on regulatory trends and reimbursement contrasted with more neutral stances that see limited upside versus the cash bid. As you read on, you will see how to track this evolving narrative and what it means for your own view on Enhabit.

Stay updated as the Fair Value for Enhabit shifts by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Enhabit.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

Earlier in 2026, UBS and TD Cowen moved to Buy ratings with price targets of US$12, pointing to clearer home health reimbursement rules, a more defined earnings outlook, and what they viewed as a discounted valuation before the deal announcement. Leerink and Truist later lifted their targets to US$13.80, aligning with the Kinderhook all cash offer and signaling that, in their view, the agreed takeout price appropriately reflected Enhabit’s execution and risk profile at that time.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

Following the Kinderhook agreement, TD Cowen, UBS, Jefferies, and Oppenheimer all shifted to Hold or Neutral, indicating they saw limited upside versus the US$13.80 cash consideration and a more balanced risk and reward trade off for new money. Truist’s Hold initiation at US$10.50 in January highlighted ongoing reimbursement and operating environment challenges, framing Enhabit as a name where execution and policy remain key watchpoints for investors even with a defined deal price on the table.

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives!NYSE:EHAB 1-Year Stock Price Chart

We've flagged 1 risk for Enhabit. See which could impact your investment.

What's in the News

Kinderhook Industries, LLC agreed to acquire Enhabit, Inc. in an all cash deal valued at about US$740 million, with stockholders set to receive US$13.80 per share under a definitive merger agreement announced on February 22, 2026. The transaction is targeted to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to Enhabit stockholder approval, Hart Scott Rodino antitrust review, regulatory clearances, and other customary closing conditions. Financing for the Kinderhook acquisition is supported by a fully committed package that includes a debt financing commitment letter from certain lenders and equity commitment letters from funds advised by Kinderhook. The merger agreement includes reciprocal termination fees. Enhabit is required to pay US$24.5 million in specified scenarios, and Kinderhook is required to pay US$44.6 million if certain termination conditions are met. Enhabit also reported goodwill impairment of US$44.7 million and intangible asset impairment of US$3 million for the fourth quarter of 2025.

Story Continues

How This Changes the Fair Value For Enhabit

Fair Value holds at US$13.80 per share, matching the agreed all cash acquisition price. Revenue Growth remains at 5.38%, with no material revision to the projected top line growth rate. Net Profit Margin is kept broadly stable at 2.32%, with no significant shift in expected profitability levels. Future P/E is maintained near 29.48x, with only a very small technical adjustment to the projected multiple. Discount Rate is adjusted slightly from 7.09% to 7.09%, indicating only a minimal refinement in the risk assumption.

Never Miss an Update: Follow The Narrative

Narratives link Enhabit’s business story to the analyst forecast and fair value, updating as new data and research come through. They help you see how qualitative drivers and risks connect to the numbers you are tracking.

Head over to the Simply Wall St Community and follow the Narrative on Enhabit to stay up to date on:

How aging demographics and the shift to in home care feed into home health and hospice demand for Enhabit. The role of payer contract renegotiations, clinical technology, and hospice expansion in supporting margins and operational efficiency. Key risks from Medicare reimbursement cuts, regulatory exposure, labor cost pressures, and consolidation among larger competitors.
